vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is partially accurate but shows significant decline. P3 (Pothos) is present with the anchor. P1 (String of Nickels) is present in the yellow pot. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are present in the black pot, though showing severe senescence.

plant_audit.P2: Systemic decline; leaf chlorosis and necrosis observed.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos cutting shows signs of dehydration; leaf edge browning.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistent moisture, no visible fungal blooms.

temporal_deltas.T-4_to_T-3: Minor postural shift in P3 leaf.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_T-1: Significant light reduction; P3 leaf appears to be entering a dormant or wilting state.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Stabilization in low light; no further structural collapse.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is under moderate stress. The Pothos (P3) is exhibiting signs of water stress or root-zone instability. The Mexican Mint (P2) is in a state of advanced senescence.
vision_report.anomalies: Presence of white perlite/debris in soil surface; no new biological introductions.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit confirms a steady decline in the health of the Pothos and Mexican Mint specimens. The environment is stable but the plants are struggling with the current light/moisture regime. The white rabbit anchor remains a consistent reference point for the P3 pot.
